title: valeriana scandens var. candolleana
intro: Valeriana scandens var. candolleana, also known as the climbing valerian, is a species of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae, characterized by its woody stems and clusters of white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being a natural sedative, anti-anxiety agent, and muscle relaxant. The health benefits of Valeriana scandens var. candolleana include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting better sleep, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia, restlessness, and muscle spasms. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the roots, stems, or leaves to make teas, tinctures, capsules, or extracts that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
